theory.canonical_statement: This entry formalizes how quantum superpositions lose coherence; curvature and zero-point fluctuations accelerate decoherence.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This identifies the two quantum states whose phase relation is being tracked.
This converts raw overlap into a scale-free coherence measure.
This closes the progression by expressing environmental loss of coherence as decay of the normalized overlap.

external_sources.title: Quantum Coherence and Decoherence
external_sources.canonical_claim: This entry formalizes how quantum superpositions lose coherence; curvature and zero-point fluctuations accelerate decoherence.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Quantum decoherence) supports the physical phenomenon (decoherence) by showing that The article explains that when a quantum system interacts with its environment, tracing out the environment leads to decoherence: off‑diagonal elements of the density matrix vanish, leaving a diagonal state.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: The article explains that when a quantum system interacts with its environment, tracing out the environment leads to decoherence: off‑diagonal elements of the density matrix vanish, leaving a diagonal state. This demonstrates how quantum superpositions lose coherence and underpins the Trawinistic description of decoherence in curved space.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.

proof_pipeline.status: source-truth provenance linked to generated proof-obligation and certificate artifacts
proof_pipeline.scope_note: This metadata records authorship, lineage, and proof-pipeline artifact links. It does not by itself assert that the entry has a completed native analytic proof.
